page: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 320 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU allocation is shared.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core performance, measured via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, falls within the average range. Memory performance is highly competitive; the PassMark memory score is in the top tier, and large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks both rank in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also demonstrate strong performance.

The AMD Genoa architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector and neural network instructions (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and hardware-accelerated cryptography (AES, VAES, SHA_NI). These features, combined with the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, make the instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, database operations, and light language model inference, where its text generation speed for small models ranks in the top 25%.
description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance includes a bundled 320 GB NVMe SSD and a single public IPv4 address. Its CPU allocation is shared, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. Multi-core performance and database operations remain in the average tier. Supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and hardware cryptography, this instance is well-suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, database hosting, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 320 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an AMD EPYC Genoa server with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 320 GB NVMe SSD, offering balanced general-purpose performance.
tagline: A balanced AMD Genoa instance featuring sixteen vCPUs, sixty-four gigabytes of RAM, and fast NVMe storage for diverse general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
64.0 GB system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Bundled 320 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark results
AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction set support
Suitable for web serving, database operations, and moderate inference
